text: The surgeon places three 8mm robotic trocars and a 12mm assistant port vertically in the right abdominal quadrant. The robot is docked from the patient's left side. The procedure begins with the opening of the left posterior rectus sheath to access the retromuscular plane. Blunt dissection is performed laterally to the linea semilunaris at the lateral border of the rectus muscle, preserving the stoma, epigastric vessels, and rectus muscle neurovascular bundles. The posterior rectus sheath is incised 0.5 cm medially to the neurovascular bundles, exposing the transversus abdominis muscle, which is released using monopolar scissors.

The surgeon gains access to the preperitoneal plane and continues dissection laterally around the lateral border of the stoma. The posterior rectal sheath is dissected along the edges of the defect, and the bowel is lateralized by incising the posterior layer. The posterior sheath is then closed around the stoma using a V-lock suture.

The surgeon measures the hernia defect intracorporeally and closes it using a barbed suture. A 15 cm lightweight partially absorbable polypropylene mesh is positioned in the retroactive space and secured with cyanoacrylate glue and absorbable stitches. The posterior rectus sheet is closed with a barbed suture.
Operative time was two hundred and twenty minutes and the patient was discharged on postoperative day two with no complications.
